title: Numbers, Time & Calendar
focus: Count, days and months, tell time, talk about my daily schedule.
function: Say and understand times, dates, and quantities in school life
grammar: It is ___ o'clock. / It is ___ thirty.
foundationalLiteracy: Number words in print alongside numerals. Writing the date in US format and recognizing that it differs from the day/month order used in most of the world.
culturalNote: Date format is a real source of error and embarrassment: 03/04 is March 4th here and April 3rd almost everywhere else. Teach it explicitly. Twelve-hour time with a.m./p.m. is also unfamiliar to students from 24-hour-clock countries. Some SLIFE students need genuine number-sense work, not just the English words — coordinate with the math teacher rather than assuming the concept is present.

1.canDo: Identify a number or a day when it is said aloud.
1.scaffold: Number line and day cards on the desk. Student points or holds up the card.
1.check: Identifies 8 of 10 spoken numbers 1–31 and 5 of 7 days.
1.l1Bridge: Check which numbers the student already holds in the home language before teaching English ones.

2.canDo: Understand a spoken time and match it to a clock.
2.scaffold: Analog and digital clock pairs. Times limited to the hour and half hour.
2.check: Matches 6 of 8 spoken times to the correct clock face.
2.l1Bridge: State explicitly, in the home language, that US clocks use 12-hour time with a.m./p.m.

3.canDo: Follow a spoken schedule change and identify what moved and when.
3.scaffold: Note-catcher with old time / new time columns. Announcement played twice.
3.check: Correctly reports the changed event and its new time and day.
3.l1Bridge: Note-catcher headings glossed; the announcement stays in English.

1.canDo: Count aloud and say the day or the time from a model.
1.scaffold: Choral counting routine. Clock manipulative in hand.
1.check: Counts 1–20 and names the current day.
1.l1Bridge: Counting may be rehearsed in the home language first to confirm number sense, then in English.

2.canDo: Say what time a class starts and what day something happens.
2.scaffold: Frame: My ___ class is at ___. / ___ is on ___. Schedule in view.
2.check: States 3 class times and days accurately from own schedule.
2.l1Bridge: Subject names glossed in the home language on the schedule.

3.canDo: Describe my week and explain a scheduling conflict.
3.scaffold: Time-preposition bank (on, at, in, before, after) and a weekly grid.
3.check: Describes a week in connected speech and explains one conflict with because.
3.l1Bridge: Reason about the scheduling conflict in the home language, explain it in English.

1.canDo: Read numerals, days, and months on a calendar.
1.scaffold: Large classroom calendar. Day and month names color-coded.
1.check: Locates today's date and names the month on a printed calendar.
1.l1Bridge: Day and month names glossed once; numerals need no translation.

2.canDo: Read a bus, bell, or lunch schedule and find a specific time.
2.scaffold: Authentic school schedule, enlarged, with the target row highlighted.
2.check: Answers 4 of 5 time questions from a real schedule.
2.l1Bridge: Schedule headings glossed in the home language.

3.canDo: Read a school calendar and identify deadlines, holidays, and early-release days.
3.scaffold: Month view with a legend. Three scenario questions.
3.check: Identifies the next no-school day and the date an assignment is due.
3.l1Bridge: Calendar legend terms (early release, no school) glossed — these are district-specific.

1.canDo: Write numerals and copy today's date correctly.
1.scaffold: Date frame with month/day/year labels above the blanks.
1.check: Writes the current date in US format without a model.
1.l1Bridge: Explicitly contrast US month/day/year order with the student's home format, in the home language.

2.canDo: Fill in a weekly schedule with times and subjects.
2.scaffold: Blank weekly grid, word bank of subjects, own schedule to copy from.
2.check: Completes a weekly grid with correct times for 5 classes.

3.canDo: Write a short plan for my week using time expressions.
3.scaffold: Preposition bank plus a model paragraph. Requires at least three time phrases.
3.check: Writes 4–5 sentences using on, at, and before/after correctly.
3.l1Bridge: Plan the week in the home language, write it in English.

1.scaffold: Points to the time on a clock manipulative and says the hour number only. Teacher expands to the full sentence and the student echoes.

2.scaffold: Frame with two slots plus the student's own printed schedule as the source of the answer.

3.say: I have [subject] at [time] on [day], so I can't come [other time]. Can I come [alternative] instead?
3.scaffold: Open frame plus a preposition bank. Uses the schedule to reason about a conflict and propose a solution.
